source: agentic-hub docs/system/PLAN-LIFECYCLE-SPECS.md (vendored; this fork cannot import agentic-hub)
note: Keep in sync with the source spec library. success_criteria/constraints are lists (matching the plan_lifecycle tool's POST payload shape).

presets.title: strata coverage
presets.subtitle: context-coverage visibility
presets.goal: Add a `strata coverage <repo_dir>` subcommand to the Strata CLI in `orchestrator/strata/` (cli.py + core.py). It walks the significant directories under `<repo_dir>` and prints a table with one row per directory showing whether it has `CONTEXT.md` / `DECISIONS.md` / `RESEARCH.md`, and whether each is stale (`reconciled_sha` behind that directory's `git log`), plus an overall coverage %. Reuse the existing `nearest_file` / `staleness` / `validate` helpers — do not reimplement them. Add unit tests in `orchestrator/tests/test_strata.py`.
success_criteria: `strata coverage <dir>` prints the per-directory table + a coverage %
constraints: edits confined to `orchestrator/strata/` and its tests

presets.title: strata lint
presets.subtitle: DECISIONS honesty-rule enforcement
presets.goal: Add a `strata lint <path>` subcommand to `orchestrator/strata/` that reads each `DECISIONS.md` entry and checks it declares an `enforcement:` value (a CI test, CODEOWNERS, hook, or IAM) OR is explicitly labelled `ADVISORY`. Print one FAIL line per non-compliant entry and exit 1 if any fail, else exit 0. Parse entries from the `<!-- strata:decision ... -->` blocks the templates use. Add unit tests in `orchestrator/tests/test_strata.py` covering a compliant file, a missing-enforcement entry, and an ADVISORY-labelled entry.
success_criteria: `strata lint` exits 1 + prints the offending entry for a decision with no enforcement and no ADVISORY label
constraints: edits confined to `orchestrator/strata/` and its tests

presets.title: strata lift
presets.subtitle: local quick lift-summary
presets.goal: Add a `strata lift <runs_dir>` subcommand to `orchestrator/strata/` that reads local RunRecord JSON (`<runs_dir>/**/record.json`) and prints a quick tally: count of first-attempt phases WITH context (`context_shas` non-empty) vs WITHOUT (empty) vs A/B-suppressed (`context_suppressed` true), and the first-pass completion rate (`status == "completed"`) per arm. Read-only. It is a lightweight LOCAL complement to army-of-one's `atlas.eval.strata_lift` (no Atlas, no network). Add unit tests with fixture record dicts in `orchestrator/tests/test_strata.py`.
success_criteria: `strata lift <dir>` over fixture records prints the WITH/WITHOUT/suppressed counts + per-arm first-pass rates
running it never modifies any file
constraints: edits confined to `orchestrator/strata/` and its tests

presets.title: Drain run summary → Slack
presets.subtitle: use case #30 / #41-C
presets.goal: After a drain run completes, post a concise summary to Slack. Add a PURE summary-builder function in `orchestrator/` that takes a list of RunRecords for a run and returns Slack-friendly text: plan_id, each phase with its status, PR URLs opened, total spend, and the WITH-vs-suppressed context tally. Wire the drain to post it to a channel from an env var, reusing the EXISTING slack-sdk client + token path already used by the deploy-approval / watchdog code (do not add a new Slack client or secret). The post must be fail-soft — a Slack error never fails the drain. Unit-test the pure builder (the Slack post is mocked).
success_criteria: the pure builder produces the correct summary from a list of RunRecords (tested)
the drain posts it to the configured channel with the Slack client mocked in tests
constraints: no new AWS resources / no terraform
reuse the existing slack-sdk client + token path

presets.title: Backlog-seed tool
presets.subtitle: Self-Building Backlog (#30)
presets.goal: Add a tool/CLI under `orchestrator/` that reads OPEN "Todo" issues for a Linear team (READ-ONLY — never mutate Linear) and emits drain-ready phase spec files under `plans/<plan_id>/phases/<plan_id>-N.md`, each with Scope / Verification Steps / Acceptance Criteria derived from the issue's title + description, so backlog items become autonomously drainable. Implement the issue→spec transformation as a PURE function and unit-test it; the Linear fetch can be mocked. Reuse the existing Linear client in `orchestrator/linear/`.
success_criteria: a pure transform turns a Linear issue dict into a valid phase-spec markdown with Scope/Verification/Acceptance (tested)
the tool writes one spec file per Todo issue under `plans/<plan_id>/phases/`
